name: TRIIM-X registry has no posted results
summary: The TRIIM-X registry shows follow-up clinical momentum for TRIIM-style neuroendocrine and epigenetic-aging endpoints, but it is not yet evidence of benefit or safety.
statement: The TRIIM-X registry lists a randomized phase 2 study with epigenetic age, thymus regeneration, safety, and immunosenescence endpoints, but ClinicalTrials.gov had no posted results in the record available on June 2, 2026.
time_horizon: Registry first posted May 5, 2020; status verified May 2025; last update posted May 7, 2025; estimated completion December 2025 in the current registry record
quantitative_note: Estimated enrollment is 85 participants, and the registry status is recruiting in the record returned on June 2, 2026.
caveats: Registry status can lag real-world study status.
The estimated completion date in the registry can be stale.
No results are posted in the registry.
The registry cannot support benefit, safety, or stage-upgrade claims without results.
